entries.title: Shakespeare
entries.content: English playwright (1564–1616). Major works: Hamlet, Romeo and Juliet, Macbeth, Othello. Influenced language and Western drama.

entries.title: Literary Devices
entries.content: Metaphor, simile, alliteration, personification, irony, foreshadowing. Tools writers use to create meaning and style.

entries.title: Sonnet Form
entries.content: 14-line poem, often iambic pentameter. Shakespearean: ABAB CDCD EFEF GG. Petrarchan: octave + sestet.

entries.title: Greek Tragedy
entries.content: Drama exploring fate and human flaw. Sophocles' Oedipus Rex, Euripides' Medea. Featured chorus and catharsis.

entries.title: Novel vs Short Story
entries.content: Novel: long prose fiction with developed plot and characters. Short story: brief, single focus, often one main conflict.
